title: quercus hartwissiana var. macrocarpa
intro: Quercus hartwissiana var. macrocarpa, also known as the Hartwiss oak or Korean evergreen oak, is a variant of the Quercus hartwissiana species, which is a type of evergreen tree native to East Asia. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues, including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The health benefits of Quercus hartwissiana var. macrocarpa include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Quercetin, a flavonoid compound extracted from the plant, is often used in herbal preparations, such as supplements, teas, and infusions, to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
